failures.symptom: Computed numbers from execute_python are stamped with [CITE:ev_*] evidence citations — the citation lane, which the mission invariant explicitly bars for computed values ('Computed numbers render ONLY through the verified lane / [#calc:]; exploratory execute_python output is BARRED from rendering'). In my real run the derived value 'mean: 0.0, n: 3' came back cited to ev_0/ev_1/ev_2, whose statements are 'eff 80 pct' / 'eff 81 pct' / 'eff 82 pct'. Neither 0.0 nor 3 appears in any cited span, so the provenance stamp is false. strict_verify check (d) should drop it; if it ever does not, a derived number renders with fabricated source attribution.

execute_python silently computes over TEXT-ONLY rows. When notebook.data_points is empty, _wrap_execute_python builds its input from evidence_store keeping ONLY {statement, evidence_id, source_url} — every numeric field is dropped. execute_python is registered requires_data=False, so the decide LLM is told it is available with zero data. In my real run the evidence carried value=80/81/82; the sandbox received rows without that key and the script computed mean=0.0 over [0,0,0] — a garbage number that still returned success=True and got citation-stamped.
failures.evidence: src/polaris_graph/tools/tool_registry.py:570  input_data = data_points[:30] if data_points else [ {statement, evidence_id, source_url} ... ]  + tool_registry.py:770 requires_data=False  -> REAL output: "{'n': 3, 'mean': 0.0, 'vals': [0, 0, 0]}"
failures.suspected_component: tool_registry._wrap_execute_python (input_data fallback) / registry ordering: nothing forces extract_numeric_data to run first

failures.symptom: OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS action-2 P1 CONFIRMED against real code: the computed value still cannot reach the decide LLM. summary_for_llm() — the only notebook read on the outline path — emits 'tool_name [OK/FAILED] (elapsed) — reasoning[:60]' and does NOT include step.result.markdown or statistics. So the agent sees 'execute_python [OK]' and learns nothing. Un-bricking the tool does not yet let a computed number reach the outline. The wheel is correctly left UNSIGNED.
failures.evidence: src/polaris_graph/tools/analysis_notebook.py:142 def summary_for_llm(...) — loop emits only f'  {step.step_number}. {step.tool_name} [{status}] ({step.elapsed_seconds:.1f}s) — {step.reasoning[:60]}'
failures.suspected_component: analysis_notebook.summary_for_llm (shared with react_agent — why the driver did not touch it)

failures.symptom: One non-reproducible run of the real drive returned error='No LLM client available' (the exact pre-fix brick) even though a client was constructed and closed. It did NOT reproduce across 3 subsequent clean runs after clearing __pycache__; most likely stale bytecode from the mutation test I had just run. Reporting it because I observed it and cannot prove it was only bytecode.
failures.evidence: RESULT success=False error='No LLM client available' / clients constructed=1 closed=[1]  — then, after `find . -name __pycache__ -prune -exec rm -rf {} +`, 3/3 runs gave success=True
failures.suspected_component: test artifact (stale __pycache__), not product code — but unproven

Note for the orchestrator: the brief's worktrees /workspace/{compose,outline_agent,outline_tooluse}_wt are root:root and read-only to uid 1000 (no sudo). The real, writable wheel worktrees are under /home/polaris/wt/. Any task template pointing at /workspace/*_wt will fail to write.

NO 429 observed — because I made ZERO OpenRouter calls. I stubbed only client.generate() (the HTTP boundary) and drove every other layer for real, so this run consumed no rate limit and no spend.
